examples.title: RISC-style Assembly for Array Sum
examples.description: Example showing load-store architecture: load values from memory to registers, perform addition in registers, store result back
examples.explanation: This demonstrates RISC load-store architecture: memory is accessed only through LOAD/STORE instructions. The ADD and SLT operations work only on registers, not memory. Each instruction performs exactly one operation.

examples.title: RISC 5-Stage Pipeline Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how instructions flow through pipeline stages
examples.explanation: This shows pipeline throughput: after filling the pipeline, one instruction completes per cycle instead of one per 5 cycles. This is the key performance advantage of RISC pipelining.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n instructions with n+4 cycles vs 5n without pipeline

examples.title: Delayed Branch Example
examples.description: Shows how delayed branch works with delay slot
examples.explanation: Delayed branch keeps the pipeline full by executing the instruction in the delay slot before the branch takes effect. The compiler can often fill this slot with a useful instruction.
examples.timeComplexity: Branch penalty reduced from 2-3 cycles to 0 cycles when delay slot filled
